source_option_exceptions.max-downloads: source-truth pref without a user-facing OptionHandler; Go tracks max-concurrent-downloads

entries.feature: Local .torrent single-file download over TCP peers
entries.notes: This claim is limited to local torrent metadata and TCP peer transfer.

entries.feature: Local Peer Discovery announcements and peer intake
entries.notes: LPD listener and announce plumbing exist, but interface selection and peer intake are not covered by offline conformance.

entries.feature: Torrent --show-files output
entries.notes: Conformance normalizes known stdout ordering differences from the reference binary.

entries.feature: Multi-file torrent --select-file and --index-out
entries.notes: Covers selection and output remapping for multi-file torrents.

entries.feature: Magnet URI download
entries.notes: Conformance covers tracker-backed ut_metadata download, metadata-only/save, pause-metadata, and the bt-save-metadata then bt-load-saved-metadata round trip against aria2c (saved torrent reused without re-fetching metadata). UDP-tracker magnet peer collection is not exercised offline.

entries.feature: pause-metadata runtime behavior for metadata downloads
entries.notes: Conformance covers the paused child request-group created after metadata retrieval in RPC mode.

entries.feature: BEP 9 / ut_metadata torrent metadata exchange
entries.notes: Conformance covers metadata bootstrap from a local peer fixture using ut_metadata.

entries.feature: DHT peer discovery feeding BitTorrent swarms
entries.notes: DHT bootstrap, periodic get-peers/announce, and PORT-message observation are wired and unit-covered for active swarms. This stays partial because cross-binary conformance has not been added and the wider IPv6/listener parity surface is still incomplete.

entries.feature: Internal split dht-entry-point host/port prefs
entries.notes: Conformance verifies both binaries accept --dht-entry-point=host:port (including ip:port and [ipv6]:port) and split it into the hidden host/port prefs without error. Runtime DHT entry-point dialing parity still belongs to the combined dht-entry-point options under bt.dht-peer-discovery.

entries.feature: Peer exchange / ut_pex
entries.notes: ut_pex reserved-bit advertising plus send/receive handling are wired for active swarms, and both fresh (added) and dropped peers are now fed to discovery, matching aria2's UTPexExtensionMessage::doReceivedAction. Stays partial because there is no cross-binary PEX-acquisition conformance fixture (would need a 3-node swarm).

entries.feature: HTTP tracker announce for local .torrent downloads
entries.notes: This does not claim UDP tracker or full tracker policy parity.

entries.feature: UDP tracker runtime use
entries.notes: The tracker dispatcher routes udp:// announces through the BitTorrent runtime, and bt-tracker-connect-timeout now bounds the announce dial (unit-tested). This stays partial because there is still no dedicated reference-vs-Go conformance fixture for UDP tracker wire behavior offline.

entries.feature: Tracker overrides, exclusion, intervals, tiers, completed/stopped announces
entries.notes: Basic metadata announce-list handling exists. Configured bt-tracker, bt-exclude-tracker, tracker timing knobs, tier policy, and completed/stopped announces are not fully wired.

entries.feature: Web seed / url-list data source
entries.notes: CLI url-list webseed download is conformance-covered and positional webseed runtime is unit-covered. Mid-download fallback and broader source-truth fallback semantics remain partial.

entries.feature: Post-completion seeding, incoming peer listener, seed-ratio and seed-time
entries.notes: aria2go now actually serves piece data as a seeder (unchokes on Interested, honors choke state), proven cross-binary by an aria2go-seeder to aria2c-leecher transfer; the post-completion seed loop honors seed-ratio and seed-time. Stays partial because bt-stop-timeout and bt-detach-seed-only behavior is not yet conformance-isolated.

entries.feature: BitTorrent MSE/PE runtime behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers encrypted transfers in both directions with bt-require-crypto: aria2c seeder to aria2go leecher and aria2go seeder to aria2c leecher, exercising both outbound and inbound MSE handshakes. Fine-grained bt-min-crypto-level negotiation edges remain unverified but the runtime crypto path is proven.

entries.feature: uTP transport
entries.notes: Runtime peer dials race TCP and uTP when a session uTP socket is available. End-to-end conformance and uTP listener bind-failure parity remain partial.

entries.feature: bt-remove-unselected-file
entries.notes: Conformance covers selecting one torrent file and removing the unselected payload files on completion.

entries.feature: HTTP single-file download
entries.notes: This row is limited to the basic local plain-HTTP payload path. HTTPS/TLS and the richer auth/header surface are tracked separately.

entries.feature: HTTPS download and TLS certificate option behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers check-certificate=false, custom CA trust, and min-tls-version where the fixture can observe it. A mutual-TLS client-certificate (certificate + private-key) conformance test exists but is environment-skipped on AppleTLS aria2c builds; it runs on OpenSSL/GnuTLS builds. Row stays partial on macOS reference builds.

entries.feature: HTTP range resume and split downloads
entries.notes: Core HTTP range/split fixtures pass, including gzip/deflate full-body parity with segmented range requests disabled for encoded responses. max-resume-failure-tries and some resume fallback edge cases still differ from source truth.

entries.feature: HTTP headers, user-agent, referer, and basic auth
entries.notes: Covers user-agent, referer, custom headers, eager basic auth, and challenge-driven auth retry behavior exercised by the offline fixtures.

entries.feature: HTTP cookies, gzip, redirects, status retry, no-proxy edge behavior
entries.notes: Grouped because the fixture matrix exercises them as HTTP edge behavior. Redirect limits, Netscape and Firefox/SQLite cookie load/save, gzip accepted output, status-retry edges, and no-proxy bypass are covered.

entries.feature: HTTP output routing, overwrite, rename, resume, conditional, remote-time and content-disposition
entries.notes: Conformance covers overwrite, auto rename, continue/resume, resume fallback to the next mirror before scratch restart, default-vs-stderr result routing, conditional requests, remote-time, and content-disposition filename handling including UTF-8 fallback policy.

entries.feature: remote-time behavior for FTP and SFTP transfers
entries.notes: FTP MDTM-derived mtime and SFTP remote mtime propagation are both exercised against aria2c and match the source-truth transfer paths covered by the local fixtures.

entries.feature: FTP passive single-file download
entries.notes: Advanced FTP option parity is tracked separately.

entries.feature: SFTP password authentication and host-key digest checks
entries.notes: Windows reference builds may skip this fixture because SFTP support is build-dependent.

entries.feature: Eager --input-file, stdin input, and per-entry options
entries.notes: Deferred input and error handling gaps are tracked separately.

entries.feature: Parameterized URI expansion
entries.notes: Covers CLI-side parameterized expansion for standalone URIs and torrent webseed inputs, plus engine-side expansion for input-file entries.

entries.feature: --input-file error behavior
entries.notes: Missing input files fail like aria2; empty and comment-only input files exit 0 on both binaries. Documented divergence: a chmod-0000 unreadable input file exits 0 in aria2c (silently treated as no entries) but exits 1 in aria2go, which intentionally surfaces the I/O error. Row stays partial for that one edge.

entries.feature: --deferred-input incremental parser
entries.notes: Conformance covers incremental queue promotion while the first entry blocks, and engine tests cover deferred parser refill behavior.

entries.feature: Local .torrent and .metalink path classification inside input/session files
entries.notes: Engine runtime tests cover classifying local .torrent and .metalink entries into metadata request groups. Cross-binary conformance coverage has not been added yet.

entries.feature: max-tries, retry-wait, and max-file-not-found across protocols
entries.notes: HTTP body-transfer retry coverage includes 404 max-file-not-found handling and 503/504 retry edges. FTP and SFTP retry loops remain incomplete.

entries.feature: --checksum, --check-integrity, realtime chunk checksum, and Metalink hash verification
entries.notes: Conformance covers CLI checksum success/failure, hash-check-only complete and incomplete behavior without metadata, Digest response-header verification, and Metalink bad-hash failure. Full --check-integrity and realtime chunk checksum parity remain partial.

entries.feature: HTTP pipelining
entries.notes: Options parse, but the HTTP driver has no pipelining mode. Documented as a deliberate, infeasible gap: HTTP/1.1 pipelining is disabled by default in aria2 (enable-http-pipelining defaults to false), Go's net/http transport cannot pipeline requests (one request per connection, or HTTP/2 multiplexing), the behavior is superseded by HTTP/2, and it is not observable in an offline black-box conformance test. Kept missing rather than implemented as a no-op.

entries.feature: lowest-speed-limit enforcement
entries.notes: Conformance covers aborting a slow HTTP transfer under lowest-speed-limit. FTP and SFTP also wire the same speed guard, but broader cross-binary coverage and adaptive-selector parity remain partial.

entries.feature: URI selector, reuse-uri, stream-piece-selector, server-stat feedback
entries.notes: Feedback/adaptive URI selection and server-stat save/load are unit-covered. Full aria2 mirror switching semantics and FTP/SFTP retry parity remain partial.

entries.feature: max-concurrent-downloads and max-connection-per-server
entries.notes: Queue concurrency is wired, and engine URI selection respects per-host caps. Broader end-to-end per-server connection parity remains partial.

entries.feature: File allocation modes
entries.notes: Allocator selection now routes none, trunc, falloc, prealloc, and no-file-allocation-limit through shared runtime paths. This stays partial because allocator semantics are still only unit-covered; many conformance fixtures use file-allocation=none as harness setup rather than proving allocator behavior.

entries.feature: Download and upload rate limit options
entries.notes: Throttle objects are wired for global/per-download limits; full protocol coverage, upload-side BitTorrent behavior, and conformance timing assertions remain partial.

entries.feature: Proxy option routing and no-proxy bypass behavior
entries.notes: HTTP proxy routing (all-proxy use, plain-HTTP absolute-form GET, tunnel CONNECT, no-proxy bypass) and ftp-proxy-specific GET plus isolation are conformance-covered. SFTP proxy (offline SFTP fixture not ready) and the full proxy option family remain partial.

entries.feature: Interface binding, IPv6 disable, DSCP, and socket receive buffer options
entries.notes: Conformance covers disable-ipv6 for HTTP and FTP (both binaries succeed against a 127.0.0.1-only fixture). DSCP marking, socket-recv-buffer sizing, and interface/multiple-interface binding are wired and unit-covered but cannot be asserted black-box offline, so this row stays partial.

entries.feature: async-dns, enable-async-dns6, and async-dns-server runtime behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers async-dns enabled with an IP target, async-dns=false fallback parity, and async-dns-server flag acceptance. Two documented gaps keep this partial: enable-async-dns6 is a deprecated no-op in aria2c (logged via A2_LOG_WARN, value discarded) but is honored in aria2go; and custom async-dns-server resolution cannot be black-box tested because c-ares only queries port 53 (no ephemeral-port DNS fixture).

entries.feature: dry-run probe behavior without writing payload files
entries.notes: Dry-run now probes the server (HTTP HEAD, FTP SIZE, SFTP stat) and completes without writing the payload, matching aria2; a dead URL fails on both binaries with equal exit codes. Conformance covers HTTP probe-success-no-file and dead-URL parity; FTP/SFTP share the same probe-then-complete path. Metadata (torrent/metalink/magnet) dry-run still short-circuits to success.

entries.feature: ftp-type, ftp-reuse-connection, proxy-method, and advanced FTP behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers ftp-type, passive/active RETR, SIZE-unsupported fallback, percent-decoded paths, proxy GET/tunnel, and ftp-reuse-connection (control connection reused across two sequential downloads with equal accept counts to aria2c). Exotic FTP auth edge cases are not separately claimed.

entries.feature: SFTP private-key formats, encrypted keys, and SSH agent
entries.notes: SSH agent support, encrypted-key parsing, and encrypted private-key passphrase auth are wired and unit-covered. This extends aria2go beyond aria2, which supports only SFTP password auth (no SSH key/agent auth), so cross-binary conformance is impossible by construction; the row stays partial as a deliberate, documented extension.

entries.feature: CLI --metalink-file HTTP-backed download
entries.notes: This claim is limited to the single-file HTTP metalink path; filters, mirrors, and hash behavior are tracked separately.

entries.feature: Metalink filters, base URI, mirror fallback, server count, and unique protocol
entries.notes: Conformance covers base-URI resolution, mirror fallback, and metalink-language/os/version file selection (show-files and download) matching aria2c. Remains partial because metalink-enable-unique-protocol exclusion and per-file server-count (maxconnections) are not yet enforced by the engine.

entries.feature: Metalink file and piece hash verification
entries.notes: Conformance covers Metalink whole-file-hash failure plus piece-hash verification (bad pieces are rejected, good pieces succeed, byte-identical output). Documented divergence keeps this partial: aria2c retries a failed piece from another mirror (DL_RETRY_EX) and exits 1 after retries, while aria2go fails immediately with the checksum exit code 32.

entries.feature: Metalink --show-files output
entries.notes: Conformance covers Metalink --show-files output with the same normalized stdout strategy used for torrent show-files.

entries.feature: Basic save-session/load-session lifecycle
entries.notes: Paused save-session reload and completion are conformance-covered. Wider option preservation and shutdown-path fidelity are tracked separately.

entries.feature: Complete aria2 option preservation in session files
entries.notes: Engine save now preserves explicit request options and metadata URIs, but it still falls well short of aria2's full session option surface.

entries.feature: Console progress and result display controls
entries.notes: Conformance covers result suppression/routing, show-console-readout/summary interaction, and download-result hide/full/default (percent column) parity. truncate-console-readout, human-readable byte formatting, and enable-color wiring remain partial.

entries.feature: --summary-interval timing
entries.notes: Conformance covers the summary banner exact text appearing with --summary-interval=1 during a blocking download, no banner with --summary-interval=0 plus readout off, and cross-binary agreement on banner presence/absence; console unit tests cover interval gating across renders.

entries.feature: CLI help and version output
entries.notes: Includes --version and -v plus full, basic, short-flag, and tagged help output, with the existing build-dependent help normalizations.

entries.feature: conf-path, no-conf, config parsing, CLI precedence
entries.notes: Covers conf-path success and failure, --no-conf, config parsing, and CLI-over-config/default precedence.

entries.feature: --stop and --stop-with-process
entries.notes: Both stop timers are exercised against blocked transfers and match aria2c's public exit-7/in-progress behavior.

entries.feature: daemon, startup-idle-time, event-poll, and rlimit-nofile lifecycle options
entries.notes: Conformance covers lowest-speed-limit abort timing and exit-code parity (the startup-idle-time grace feeds this guard). Stays partial: --startup-idle-time is a hidden CLI option that aria2c rejects (exit 28) while aria2go accepts it; daemon double-fork detachment and selectable event-poll backends are not behaviorally testable offline.

entries.feature: Signal shutdown, force escalation, and save-session on exit
entries.notes: Signal-driven shutdown preserves active and waiting entries and saves them in matching order (active before waiting) on SIGTERM. Stays partial: with the RPC saveSession path, aria2c serializes an unstarted download via unfinishedResults (before active) while aria2go writes active-before-waiting; staged signal escalation is also unverified.

entries.feature: on-download-start/complete/error hook argument behavior
entries.notes: Covers the hook argument behavior exercised by offline fixtures.

entries.feature: on-download-pause, on-download-stop, and on-bt-download-complete hooks
entries.notes: Conformance covers pause-hook delivery on an active download, stop-vs-error behavior on signal shutdown, and the BitTorrent completion hook.

entries.feature: RPC notification name registry and WebSocket delivery
entries.notes: Covers advertised notification names plus WebSocket delivery of runtime notifications from the dispatcher bridge.

entries.feature: Advertised RPC method registry
entries.notes: Conformance covers the advertised method set and exact listMethods ordering. Method semantics are tracked by the behavior-specific RPC rows.

entries.feature: addUri, tellStatus, tellActive/tellWaiting/tellStopped, queue controls
entries.notes: Covers core queue and status behavior, including tellWaiting negative offsets and removing a waiting download without creating a stopped result. Full stopped-result fidelity still differs from source truth.

entries.feature: getFiles, getUris, getServers for non-BT/HTTP active downloads
entries.notes: Active non-BT HTTP paths are covered, including the missing-GID error shape for getServers. Stopped and multi-file views are still synthesized differently from source-truth request-group/file-entry state.

entries.feature: addTorrent/addMetalink uploaded metadata validation and save behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers uploaded torrent and metalink validation, paused enqueue behavior, and rpc-save-upload-metadata session persistence. XML-RPC upload normalization is tracked under rpc.transports rather than claimed here.

entries.feature: aria2.addMetalink multi-file enqueue and multi-GID return semantics
entries.notes: Conformance covers returning one distinct GID per generated Metalink request group while ignoring a caller-supplied gid override.

entries.feature: RPC secret auth and read-only method gating
entries.notes: Covers token auth and read-only denial behavior.

entries.feature: RPC TLS transport using rpc-secure, rpc-certificate, and rpc-private-key
entries.notes: Conformance exercises secure HTTP and secure WebSocket transport with rpc-certificate. A separate rpc-certificate + rpc-private-key PEM-pair conformance test exists but is environment-skipped on AppleTLS aria2c builds (which manage trust via the OS keychain), so this row remains partial on macOS reference builds; it runs on OpenSSL/GnuTLS builds.

entries.feature: JSON-RPC HTTP POST/GET JSONP, XML-RPC, batch, multicall, WebSocket notifications
entries.notes: HTTP POST, GET JSONP, XML-RPC, multicall, WebSocket notifications, fragmented WebSocket messages, oversized-request abort, and XML-RPC uploaded-metadata normalization (addTorrent via base64 element returns a valid GID with a working tellStatus, matching aria2c) are all conformance-covered.

entries.feature: JSON-RPC and WebSocket batch per-entry error behavior
entries.notes: Conformance covers mixed-validity and invalid-object batch errors over HTTP/JSONP plus the WebSocket missing-id per-entry error shape.

entries.feature: Stopped-result file, URI, bitfield, and status fidelity
entries.notes: Stopped tellStatus now omits the seeder key for terminal statuses, matching aria2's gatherStoppedDownload (verified against RpcMethodImpl.cc). Stays partial because Go stores reduced stopped records: the bittorrent metadata dict and the full file/URI/bitfield surface are not preserved for stopped downloads.

entries.feature: RPC edge-case parameter validation
entries.notes: Conformance covers mixed-type URI filtering on addUri, missing-GID getOption errors, and stopped-download changeOption rejection. Some broader malformed-parameter coercions still differ.

entries.feature: RPC option validation parity
entries.notes: Conformance covers changeGlobalOption/addUri option validation edges against aria2c: invalid numeric and invalid enum values (max-concurrent-downloads, bt-min-crypto-level, log-level) return the same error code and message, split=0 is rejected, and unknown options are silently ignored, all matching aria2c.

entries.feature: changeGlobalOption runtime side effects
entries.notes: Some values merge into config but already-created runtime state such as throttles is not fully rewired.

entries.feature: aria2.getPeers with active BitTorrent peer details
entries.notes: Non-BitTorrent behavior and the missing-GID error shape are covered. Active BitTorrent peer detail is still not implemented.
